---
title: "Install Python and R in VScode"
author: "Data Analysis with R"
date: "July 7, 2025"
---

Set up Python

```{python}

import os
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path

venv_path = Path(".venv")

# Find Python
import sys
python_path = sys.executable  

# Create or reuse venv
if not venv_path.exists():
    print("Creating virtual environment...")
    subprocess.run([python_path, "-m", "venv", ".venv"])
else:
    print("Using existing virtual environment...")

# Install pip packages
pip_path = venv_path / "bin" / "pip"

import os
print(os.getcwd())

requirements = Path("./training_site/requirements.txt")

subprocess.run([pip_path, "install", "--upgrade", "pip"])

if requirements.exists():
    subprocess.run([pip_path, "install", "-r", "./training_site/requirements.txt"])
else:
    print("No requirements.txt found.")

# Show Python version
python_exec = venv_path / "bin" / "python"
subprocess.run([python_exec, "--version"])
```

Set up R

```{r}

options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cran.rstudio.com/"))

if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("renv")
}

# Use existing renv environment if renv.lock exists; otherwise init
if (file.exists("renv.lock")) {
  renv::restore(prompt = FALSE)
} else {
  renv::init(bare = TRUE)  # avoids reinstalling base packages
}

# Define required packages
required_pkgs <- c(
  "rmarkdown", "gapminder", "tidyverse", "readxl",
  "openxlsx", "gridExtra", "ggExtra", "knitr","reticulate"
)

# Install missing packages
missing_pkgs <- setdiff(required_pkgs, installed.packages()[,"Package"])
if (length(missing_pkgs) > 0) {
  install.packages(missing_pkgs)
}

# Load packages quietly
invisible(lapply(required_pkgs, function(pkg) {
  suppressPackageStartupMessages(
    try(library(pkg, character.only = TRUE), silent = TRUE)
  )
}))

# Snapshot the current state
renv::snapshot(prompt = FALSE)

```
